mappings.rationaleAI: The Great Stink directly covers London's sanitation crisis and Joseph Bazalgette's sewer engineering solution (A.2.a), the water-supply implications (A.1), and the intersection of public health and urban infrastructure addressing urban pollution (C.1).
The Great Fire of London and subsequent development of fire regulations and insurance directly addresses fire prevention and control technology in urban settings (A.6).

Covers the explosive growth of industrial cities, urban planning, social problems of urbanization including overcrowding and disease, modernist architecture, and contemporary challenges of urban sustainability — spanning water, sanitation, transportation, recreational facilities, and pollution control.

Examines 20th-century urban developments including the automobile's impact on city design, suburban expansion, modernist urban planning visions, and the sustainable/smart city concept — relevant to urban transportation (A.3), recreational facilities (B.2), and responses to urban problems (C.1).

Covers the history of cities from Bronze Age origins including urban infrastructure functions like trade, governance, and the basic engineering needs of early urban settlements — relevant to foundational urban engineering services.

London's history encompasses urban development including water supply, sanitation, transportation systems, and the Great Fire's impact on fire prevention — covering multiple basic engineering services of a major city.
